rule_text: When he wants to contact someone about work, community projects, or plans, he prefers the message to be drafted for review instead of being sent immediately.
canonical_answer: I like messages drafted first so I can review the tone before anything gets sent.
counterfactual_default: Send the message directly once the requested content is ready.

evidence.content: maintains a reflective journal to manage his emotional reactivity

rule_text: When triaging incoming messages tied to mentorship, client work, or community organizing, he prefers to mark them high priority so they do not get lost.
canonical_answer: I mark mentorship, client, and community-project messages as high priority so I stay on top of them.
counterfactual_default: Leave the messages at normal priority and rely on the inbox order.

rule_text: When he is keeping up an existing checklist or workshop log, he prefers recording the next action rather than just changing the status.
canonical_answer: On an existing tracker, I like to record the next action so the follow-up stays concrete.
counterfactual_default: Simply update the status field and leave the next step unspecified.

evidence.content: emphasising clear documentation and collaborative problem solving

rule_text: When an invitation overlaps with something unresolved or potentially tiring, he prefers to respond tentatively rather than accepting or declining right away.
canonical_answer: If an invite conflicts with my energy or plans, I usually answer tentatively first.
counterfactual_default: Accept or decline the invitation outright based on the current best guess.

evidence.content: bouts of anxiety that sometimes derail their disciplined routines

rule_text: When dining out or meeting socially, he prefers reservations at culturally rooted local places over generic mainstream options.
canonical_answer: For meals out, I prefer local places with cultural character over generic spots.
counterfactual_default: Choose a convenient or widely rated restaurant without prioritizing local cultural character.

rule_text: When booking local service appointments, he prefers familiar, community-oriented providers over anonymous chain-style options.
canonical_answer: For local appointments, I prefer someone rooted in the community over a generic chain provider.
counterfactual_default: Choose the most available or most broadly rated provider regardless of community connection.

evidence.content: The close-knit community of Villalba Municipio and the broader Puerto Rican diaspora inform his values of humility, cooperation, and cultural pride.

rule_text: When selecting stops for a trip, he prefers culturally rich, artisanal, or historic places over generic tourist highlights.
canonical_answer: I choose stops with history, craft, and local culture rather than generic attractions.
counterfactual_default: Choose the most popular or highest-rated tourist stops regardless of cultural depth.

rule_text: When a booked trip is disrupted, he prefers replacing failed stops with alternatives in the same cultural or nature theme rather than swapping the whole trip.
canonical_answer: If part of a trip falls through, I prefer a like-for-like replacement that keeps the trip's original character.
counterfactual_default: Replace the full itinerary or choose any convenient substitute regardless of theme.

rule_text: For long-distance travel, he prefers ground transport over flights when that option fits the trip.
canonical_answer: When I can, I prefer going by ground rather than flying.
counterfactual_default: Look for and book flights first for long-distance travel.

evidence.content: A deliberate traveler focused on modest savings and calmer travel would often favor the slower, less hectic option over flying.

rule_text: When a flight must be booked, he prefers a window seat for the calmer view and quieter experience.
canonical_answer: If I have to fly, I prefer a window seat.
counterfactual_default: Choose an aisle seat for convenience getting up and down.

evidence.content: often capturing sunrise vistas with his DSLR camera

rule_text: When booking lodging, he prefers offers that match modest, budget-conscious stays over upscale accommodations.
canonical_answer: I book modest places that fit the budget instead of paying for upscale lodging.
counterfactual_default: Choose the most comfortable or premium hotel offer within reach.

evidence.content: plan each trip around modest savings
staying with family in nearby coastal villages when budgets tighten

rule_text: When shopping for food or cooking supplies, he prefers products suited to traditional Puerto Rican cooking over generic alternatives.
canonical_answer: For cooking, I choose ingredients that fit Puerto Rican dishes and flavors.
counterfactual_default: Pick generic or broadly popular products without regard to Puerto Rican cooking traditions.

evidence.content: preparing traditional dishes like arroz con gandules, pasteles, and lechón de cerdo
relish seasoning with sofrito, cilantro, and adobo

rule_text: When returning a purchased item that did not work out, he prefers an exchange over a refund so he can still get the right version.
canonical_answer: If something misses the mark, I usually want an exchange rather than just a refund.
counterfactual_default: Request a refund and end the purchase altogether.

evidence.content: preparing traditional dishes like arroz con gandules, pasteles, and lechón de cerdo, often adding modern twists
His maker mentality and iterative habits suggest replacing an imperfect item with a better-fit version rather than abandoning the purchase.
